strategic_profile.executive_summary: The Catholic University of America (CUA) is a private, pontifical research university located in Washington, D.C., uniquely positioned as the national university of the Catholic Church in the U.S. Classified as an R1 research institution, it heavily emphasizes a liberal arts curriculum rooted in the Catholic intellectual tradition alongside strong professional programs in nursing, architecture, and social work. Its location in the 'Little Rome' neighborhood provides students with unparalleled access to federal internships, political networks, and national institutions.

analyst_takeaways.insight: CUA integrates philosophy and theology extensively into its core curriculum across all majors.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should be prepared for a traditional, faith-integrated liberal arts education, even within STEM and business programs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 84% of undergraduates self-identify as Catholic, and campus life is heavily influenced by Catholic social teaching, ministries, and traditions.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university provides a highly supportive environment for traditional Catholic students, but may present cultural adjustments for non-Catholic or highly liberal students.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 94% to 99% of students receive institutional grants or scholarships, with average aid amounts significantly reducing the high sticker price.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The published cost of attendance is over $81,000, but generous merit and parish-based scholarships make the actual net price competitive for strong applicants.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: The social scene is deeply community-oriented, heavily anchored by residence hall life, campus ministries, and over 100 student clubs. While there is no officially recognized Greek life, a few independent Greek organizations exist. The university promotes alcohol-free programming on weekends.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located on a 176-acre campus in the Brookland neighborhood, students have direct Metro access, making it incredibly easy to commute into downtown D.C. for internships and entertainment.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution, CUA offers strong undergraduate research opportunities, particularly in engineering, physics, and social sciences, often in collaboration with nearby federal agencies.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional since 2020-2021 for all undergraduate applicants, including international and home-schooled students. Standardized tests are not reviewed for admission, the University Honors Program, or merit scholarships.

gpa.notes: Archdiocesan Scholarship requires at least a 3.8 unweighted GPA.

testing.policy_note: Test-optional policy allows students full consideration for admission and merit scholarships without scores.

colleges.name: Conway School of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking an elite clinical nursing education integrated with Catholic biomedical ethics. Extremely highly ranked.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, clinically focused students dedicated to serving in healthcare fields.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.notes: Consistently ranked among top undergraduate nursing programs in the US.

colleges.name: Busch School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Perfect for students looking to study business through the lens of Catholic social principles. Features a unique 3-Year Accelerated B.S.B.A program.
colleges.student_archetype: Ethically-minded future corporate leaders and entrepreneurs.

majors.name: Business Administration - Strategy, Management, and Operations
majors.notes: Students can opt into a 3-Year accelerated track.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking a classic liberal arts education with close proximity to Capitol Hill for political science and history majors.
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually curious students interested in humanities, public service, and research.

majors.name: Politics
majors.notes: Highly popular due to the university's D.C. location and strong Capitol Hill alumni network.

colleges.name: National Catholic School of Social Service
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing social work grounded in Catholic teachings on human dignity.
colleges.student_archetype: Service-oriented students looking to enter clinical or community social work.

majors.name: Social Work
majors.notes: Ranked among the top Catholic social work programs in the nation.

supplemental_requirements.details: Archdiocesan Scholarship finalists require an interview and a 250-word statement of interest.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: The admissions committee values high school rigor and GPA over standardized test scores, which are entirely optional. The university explicitly seeks students committed to service and faith-based community building.

major_selection_tactics: Indicate your parish affiliation on the Common Application to automatically trigger consideration for the $4,000 Parish Scholarship.
Submit a comprehensive service resume by January 15th to be considered for the full-tuition O'Connell Service Scholarship.
Consider the 3-Year B.S.B.A. program in the Busch School of Business for an accelerated, cost-effective degree path.

scholarships.name: Archdiocesan Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition for four years.

scholarships.name: The Catholic University Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable four-year scholarship for exceptional academic profiles.

scholarships.name: Parish Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable four-year scholarship for parishioners of Catholic churches in the US.

scholarships.name: Legacy Grant
scholarships.benefits: Renewable grant for students with a parent, grandparent, or sibling who graduated from CUA.

scholarships.name: Most Reverend David M. O'Connell Service Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Four-year full tuition for a student from the Diocese of Trenton with a demonstrated record of service.

ap_policy.general_rule: Credits are typically awarded for AP exam scores of 4 or 5.
exceptions: ENG 101 (English Composition) - No AP credit given
ap_policy.usage: Can satisfy liberal arts core requirements or count toward major prerequisites.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credits are awarded for Higher Level (HL) exams completed as part of the full IB Diploma program.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Dual-enrollment courses must be taken at a regionally accredited institution; classes taught at a high school by a high school instructor do not qualify.

what_it_takes: Demonstrated commitment to Catholic values or community service
Unweighted GPA over 3.5 for standard admission, or 3.8+ for top scholarships
common_activities: Service organizations (e.g., DC Reads, Best Buddies)
essay_tips: If Catholic, emphasize your parish involvement as it aligns with the university's mission.
Focus on intellectual curiosity blending faith and reason.
red_flags: Highly secular or progressive students may find the mandatory philosophy/theology core and traditional Catholic cultural enforcement jarring.
Expectations of a traditional state-school party or Greek life scene (CUA has no officially recognized Greek chapters and strict dorm policies).
insights: The 'D.C. Advantage' is real; CUA's location gives students massive leverage for political and federal internships.
The university maintains single-room waitlists due to high demand for specific upperclassmen housing.
Classes are generally small (11:1 student-faculty ratio), ensuring heavy interaction with professors.
